words.definition: a set of rules a computer follows to perform a task
example_sentences: The search engine uses an algorithm to rank results.
Social media algorithms decide what content you see.
The algorithm was updated and traffic dropped significantly.
words.pronunciation_tip: AL-guh-rith-um — four syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'the computer programme decides' — say 'the algorithm decides'.

words.definition: the amount of data that can be transmitted over a network
example_sentences: Streaming HD video requires a lot of bandwidth.
Our bandwidth is limited so video calls are slow.
The company upgraded its bandwidth to handle more traffic.
words.pronunciation_tip: BAND-width — stress on first syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'internet speed' and 'bandwidth' interchangeably — bandwidth is capacity, speed is rate.

words.definition: the process of converting data into a code to prevent unauthorised access
example_sentences: End-to-end encryption means only you can read your messages.
All financial data is protected by strong encryption.
The encryption was so strong it could not be broken.
words.pronunciation_tip: en-KRIP-shun — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'the data is hidden' — say 'the data is encrypted'.

words.definition: the point where two systems or a user and a system interact
example_sentences: The user interface is clean and easy to navigate.
The software has an intuitive interface that anyone can use.
They redesigned the interface to make it more accessible.
words.pronunciation_tip: IN-ter-fayss — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'the screen design' — say 'the user interface' or 'UI'.

words.definition: software designed to damage or disable computers
example_sentences: The system was infected with malware.
Always use antivirus software to protect against malware.
The malware stole thousands of passwords.
words.pronunciation_tip: MAL-wair — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'a computer virus' for all malicious software — malware is the general term.

words.definition: security software that blocks unauthorised access to a network
example_sentences: The firewall prevented the hackers from getting in.
Make sure your firewall is up to date.
The company invested in a powerful firewall system.
words.pronunciation_tip: FYR-wawl — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'security system' only — say 'firewall' for network protection.

words.definition: saving data on internet servers rather than a local device
example_sentences: I back up all my photos to cloud storage automatically.
Cloud storage means you can access files from anywhere.
The company moved all its data to cloud storage last year.
words.pronunciation_tip: KLOWD STAWR-ij.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I save online' — say 'I use cloud storage'.
words.collocations: ['cloud storage service', 'move to cloud storage', 'store in the cloud']

words.definition: computer systems able to perform tasks requiring human intelligence
example_sentences: Artificial intelligence is transforming the way businesses operate.
AI can now write text, create images, and hold conversations.
The ethical implications of artificial intelligence are complex.
words.pronunciation_tip: ar-tih-FISH-ul in-TEL-ih-junss.
words.common_mistake: In everyday use, say 'AI' (AY-EYE) not the full phrase every time.

words.definition: causing major changes to an established industry or technology
example_sentences: Smartphones were disruptive technology that changed everything.
The start-up had a disruptive approach to banking.
Disruptive innovation can make existing businesses obsolete.
words.pronunciation_tip: dis-RUP-tiv — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'it changed everything' — say 'it was disruptive technology'.

words.definition: able to grow in size or capacity to meet increasing demand
example_sentences: They need a scalable solution that can handle more users.
The cloud architecture is highly scalable.
A scalable business model is essential for growth.
words.pronunciation_tip: SKAY-luh-bul — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'can grow' — say 'scalable' in tech and business contexts.

words.definition: an error in a computer programme
example_sentences: There's a bug in the code that causes it to crash.
The developers fixed several bugs in the latest update.
Report any bugs you find using the feedback button.
words.pronunciation_tip: BUG — one syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'mistake in the programme' — say 'bug'.

words.definition: a copy of data saved separately in case the original is lost
example_sentences: Always back up your work in case the computer crashes.
I lost everything because I hadn't made a backup.
The system creates an automatic backup every night.
words.pronunciation_tip: BAK-up — stress on first syllable.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'a copy of my files' always — say 'a backup' or 'back up your data'.

words.definition: measures taken to protect computers and networks from attack
example_sentences: Cybersecurity is a growing priority for every business.
The company hired a cybersecurity expert after the breach.
Poor cybersecurity can have devastating consequences.
words.pronunciation_tip: sy-ber-sih-KYOOR-ih-tee — six syllables, stress on fourth.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'internet safety' — say 'cybersecurity' in professional contexts.

words.definition: the use of technology to perform tasks without human input
example_sentences: Automation has replaced many factory jobs.
The company invested in automation to reduce costs.
Automation increases efficiency but reduces employment.
words.pronunciation_tip: aw-tuh-MAY-shun — four syllables, stress on third.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'machines do the work' — say 'automation'.

words.definition: a new, improved version of something
example_sentences: I need to upgrade my phone — it's very slow now.
The software upgrade fixed several security issues.
The company is planning a major infrastructure upgrade.
words.pronunciation_tip: UP-grayd — stress on first syllable (noun), up-GRAYD (verb).
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'I buy a newer version' — say 'I upgrade'.

words.definition: an attempt to trick someone into revealing passwords or financial information
example_sentences: She received a phishing email pretending to be from her bank.
Phishing attacks are becoming increasingly sophisticated.
Never click links in emails — it could be phishing.
words.pronunciation_tip: FISH-ing — same pronunciation as 'fishing'.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'a fake email to steal information' — say 'a phishing attack'.

words.definition: a delay in the transmission of data between systems
example_sentences: High latency makes online gaming frustrating.
The new connection has very low latency.
Latency is different from bandwidth — it measures delay, not capacity.
words.pronunciation_tip: LAY-ten-see — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'the delay in my internet' — say 'high latency'.

words.definition: when private information is accessed or stolen illegally
example_sentences: The company suffered a major data breach affecting millions.
A data breach can destroy customer trust overnight.
She was notified that her data had been exposed in a breach.
words.pronunciation_tip: DAY-tuh BREECH.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'they stole the data' — say 'there was a data breach'.
words.collocations: ['suffer a data breach', 'major data breach', 'data breach notification']

words.definition: a computer-generated simulation of an environment
example_sentences: Virtual reality headsets are becoming more affordable.
VR is used in training, gaming, and medicine.
She tried virtual reality for the first time at the exhibition.
words.pronunciation_tip: VUR-chyoo-ul ree-AL-ih-tee.
words.common_mistake: In everyday use, say 'VR' (VEE-AR) rather than the full phrase.

words.definition: to repeat a process, making improvements each time
example_sentences: The development team iterates quickly based on user feedback.
We iterated on the design three times before launch.
Iterating is fundamental to agile software development.
words.pronunciation_tip: IT-er-ayt — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Don't say 'we improved it again' — say 'we iterated on the design'.
